fact: June 22 is almost always the longest day of the year in the Northern Hemisphere - the sun stays up for over 17 hours in some places!
In Antarctica, it's the middle of winter and the sun doesn't rise at all - it's been dark for months!
Fireflies typically start flashing more frequently around this time in North America because it's peak mating season - they're having glow-in-the-dark dance parties!
Many flowers often smell the strongest around this time because they're trying to attract bees and butterflies before summer gets too hot.
In northern Alaska, the sun won't set today - it just rolls around the sky like a giant glowing marble that never drops below the horizon!
June 22 falls exactly 173 days into the year, which means there are only 192 days left - we're past the halfway point!
Mosquitoes are typically at their most active right now in many regions because the warm, humid weather makes them breed in puddles and ponds.
In the Southern Hemisphere, this is usually the shortest day of winter - some places only get 8 hours of sunlight!
The zodiac sign switches from Gemini to Cancer around today.
Ocean water temperatures often hit their perfect swimming zone in many coastal areas because the sun has been heating them up for months.
Strawberries are typically bursting with the most sugar and juice right now in northern climates - that's why strawberry festivals happen in late June!
In Scandinavia, the sun barely sets around this time - they call it White Nights and it can mess with everyone's sleep schedule!
